message:
feat(buffer): implement optimized buffer with scissor clipping and opacity

This commit adds the OptimizedBuffer system, the core rendering surface
that holds terminal cells and provides clipping, opacity, and drawing
operations for compositing UI layers.

src/buffer/mod.rs - OptimizedBuffer:
Structure:
- width/height dimensions
- cells: Vec<Cell> linear storage (row-major, w*h size)
- scissor_stack: Nested clipping regions
- opacity_stack: Multiplicative alpha modifiers
- id: String for debugging

Core Operations:
- new(width, height): Create with empty cells
- resize(width, height): Recreate with new dimensions
- get(x, y) -> Option<&Cell>: Read cell at position
- set(x, y, cell): Write cell (respects scissor/opacity)
- set_blended(x, y, cell): Alpha-composite cell over existing
- clear(bg): Fill entire buffer with background color
- fill_rect(x, y, w, h, bg): Fill rectangular region

Scissor Integration:
- All writes check is_within_scissor() before modifying
- Out-of-bounds or clipped writes are silently ignored
- Enables nested UI viewports without manual clipping

Opacity Integration:
- Writes apply current_opacity() multiplier to cell alpha
- Enables fade effects and layered transparency

Memory Layout:
- Contiguous cell array for SIMD-friendly access
- ~32 bytes per cell (content, colors, attrs, link)
- 80x24 buffer ≈ 61KB, 200x50 ≈ 320KB

src/buffer/scissor.rs - Scissor Stack:
ClipRect struct:
- x, y: i32 (signed for off-screen regions)
- width, height: u32

ScissorStack:
- stack: Vec<ClipRect> of saved states
- current: Active clipping region

Operations:
- push(rect): Intersect with current, save previous
- pop(): Restore previous state
- is_within(x, y): Check if point is inside current clip
- current(): Get active clipping region

Intersection Algorithm:
  new_x = max(current.x, rect.x)
  new_y = max(current.y, rect.y)
  new_right = min(current.right, rect.right)
  new_bottom = min(current.bottom, rect.bottom)
  new_width = max(0, new_right - new_x)
  new_height = max(0, new_bottom - new_y)

Use Case: Nested viewports, scroll regions, modal dialogs

src/buffer/opacity.rs - Opacity Stack:
OpacityStack:
- stack: Vec<f32> of saved opacity values
- current: f32 multiplicative product

Operations:
- push(opacity): Multiply current, save previous
- pop(): Restore previous value
- current(): Get effective opacity (product of all pushed)

Multiplication Chain:
  Initial: 1.0
  push(0.5) -> current = 0.5
  push(0.5) -> current = 0.25
  pop() -> current = 0.5
  pop() -> current = 1.0

Use Case: Fade-in/out animations, disabled UI elements, overlays

src/buffer/drawing.rs - High-Level Drawing:
Text Drawing (draw_text):
- Iterates grapheme clusters using unicode module
- Allocates cells for display width (1 or 2)
- Handles wide characters with Continuation cells
- Applies style (fg, bg, attributes)
- Respects scissor clipping per-character

Box Drawing (draw_box):
- BoxStyle: ASCII, Light, Heavy, Double, Rounded
- Draws corners and edges using box-drawing characters
- Optional title with alignment (left, center, right)
- Custom character sets supported

Buffer Compositing (draw_buffer):
- Draws one buffer onto another at offset
- Respects target's scissor and opacity
- Alpha blending per cell
- Use case: Popup windows, floating panels

Line Drawing:
- draw_hline, draw_vline for separators
- draw_line for arbitrary lines (Bresenham)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
